projects.title: Flowplace: Role Compass
projects.category: SaaS Product Engineering / Professional Case Study
projects.short_description: Talent profile feature for a SaaS platform, built with Vue/Nuxt and Directus and adopted by 5,000+ users in the first month.
projects.long_description: A professional SaaS case study focused on end-to-end product engineering, responsive UI workflows, API integration, feature delivery, and sustainable frontend architecture in a high-velocity Agile team.
projects.role: Full-stack developer working on end-to-end product engineering for a core talent profile feature.
highlights: Reached 5,000+ users in the first month.
Led end-to-end development of a core talent profile feature.
Improved usability, accessibility, and consistency across product workflows.
Worked inside Agile delivery with production release discipline.
outcome_or_proof: 5,000+ users in the first month.
Professional SaaS feature in production context.
source_notes: Derived from app/composables/useProjects.ts, docs, and the Flowplace case study page.

projects.title: OpenMe
projects.category: SaaS MVP / AI-Assisted Platform / Full-Stack Product
projects.short_description: Actionable bio/profile platform where people define what others can do with them through public endpoints such as collaboration, questions, feedback, and ideas.
projects.long_description: OpenMe rethinks the personal link-in-bio page as an interaction menu. Instead of only listing links, a profile exposes clear public endpoints like collaborate, ask me, feedback, and offer help. The MVP includes profile links, custom endpoints, boundaries, inbox flows, and AI-assisted submission summaries.
projects.role: Builder and full-stack developer for a SaaS MVP with AI-assisted workflow features.
highlights: Full-stack monorepo architecture with pnpm workspaces and Turbo.
Public profile and endpoint system with authenticated owner flows.
Dashboard and inbox workflow with AI-assisted summaries for incoming submissions.
Production deployment across Vercel, Render, and Neon.
outcome_or_proof: Live deployment exists.
GitHub repository is available.
No user/adoption metric is present in the repository.

projects.title: Decision Matrix AI
projects.category: AI Product Tool / Decision Support / Full-Stack App
projects.short_description: AI-assisted decision matrix app for comparing complex choices using weighted criteria, scores, explainable recommendations, and action checklists.
projects.long_description: Decision Matrix AI helps users turn vague decisions into structured comparisons. It supports weighted criteria, must-have requirements, editable scores, ranked results, category breakdowns, AI-generated suggestions, recommendation summaries, and practical next-step checklists.
projects.role: Builder and full-stack developer for an AI-assisted decision-support product tool.
highlights: AI-generated criteria, options, scores, review, recommendations, and action checklists.
User-specific cloud persistence with Firebase and Firestore.
Import/export JSON backups for decision data.
Serverless AI calls through Netlify Functions with Zod validation.
outcome_or_proof: Live deployment exists.
GitHub repository is available.
No user/adoption metric is present in the repository.

projects.title: ARI Motors: EV Portal
projects.category: Full-Stack Web Platform / Professional Case Study
projects.short_description: Electric vehicle comparison portal built from scratch to production in under three months as a solo full-stack developer.
projects.long_description: A professional case study covering frontend interface work, backend systems, database architecture, responsive design, and a fast production launch for an electric commercial vehicle comparison portal.
projects.role: Solo full-stack developer responsible for frontend, backend, database, data handling, and launch work.
highlights: Solo full-stack delivery from concept to production.
Built vehicle comparison workflows for complex technical data.
Managed frontend, backend, data handling, and launch work.
Delivered under a tight three-month timeline.
outcome_or_proof: Launched from concept to production in under three months.
Case study page states the platform launched successfully within the deadline.
source_notes: Derived from app/composables/useProjects.ts, CV, and the ARI Motors case study page.
The current route file is leanera.vue, but its page content is ARI Motors: EV Comparison Portal.

projects.title: AI Compliance Compass
projects.category: Responsible AI Tool / Practical Compliance UX
projects.short_description: Educational AI use-case checker that turns plain-language descriptions into practical EU/Germany compliance prompts and next steps.
projects.long_description: AI Compliance Compass is a practical portfolio tool for exploring AI compliance, safety, and responsible use patterns in a German/EU context. It avoids legal certainty and focuses on educational classification, clarification questions, and next-step checklists.
projects.role: Builder of a responsible-AI product workflow inside the portfolio.
highlights: Plain-language AI use-case intake.
Clarification flow for missing context.
Educational risk, GDPR, copyright, and transparency checks.
Built-in disclaimers to avoid legal overclaiming.
outcome_or_proof: Implemented as a live route in the portfolio.
No external user/adoption metric is present in the repository.

projects.title: User Management App
projects.short_description: Full-stack user administration dashboard with FastAPI, Vue 3, CRUD workflows, search, sorting, pagination, validation, and Docker support.
projects.long_description: A practical admin dashboard focused on efficient user management workflows. It includes real-time CRUD-style interactions, smart username generation, debounced global search, sorting, pagination, inline validation, reusable Vue components, and a FastAPI backend.
highlights: Full-stack frontend/backend structure.
User CRUD operations with validation and data integrity rules.
Debounced global search, pagination, and sorting.
Docker-based setup with reusable Vue component architecture.
outcome_or_proof: GitHub repository is available.
No live URL or user/adoption metric is present in the repository.
